content.problemStatement:
You are given a convex polygon with NNN sides. You have to answer QQQ queries. The ithi^{th}ith query is described by two integers vi,tiv_i, t_ivi​,ti​. In this query, all sides of the polygon start moving parallel to their respective perpendicular vector away from the centroid with a constant velocity of viunitssecv_i \frac{units}{sec}vi​secunits​. Output the final area covered by NNN sides of the polygon after time tit_iti​ seconds.

For each query,  consider the initial coordinates of vertices of the given polygon.

**Note:**


Since the input-output is large, prefer using fast input-output methods.
The problem requires high precision, so prefer using data types with a precision equivalent to long double in C++.

Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each testcase contains N+Q+1N + Q + 1N+Q+1 lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N,QN, QN,Q.
NNN  lines follow. For each valid iii, the ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains two space-separated integers xi,yix_i, y_ixi​,yi​, coordinates of the ithi^{th}ith vertex of the polygon.
Next QQQ lines follow. For each valid iii, the ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains two space-separated integers  vi,tiv_i, t_ivi​,ti​, description of the ithi^{th}ith query.


Output Format
For each query, output in a single line the final area covered by NNN sides of the polygon.


Your answer will be considered correct if its **relative** or **absolute** error does not exceed 10−210^{-2}10−2.
constraints: The sum NNN over all testcases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105.
The sum QQQ over all testcases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105.
The vertices of polygon are given in counter-clockwise order.
The internal angles of the given polygon are greater than 1∘1^{\circ}1∘.
